content.what: L.E.O. (Living Ecosystem Orchestrator) — a living knowledge infrastructure where humans and agents think, decide, and evolve together. This repo is the core — a Go CLI tool that bootstraps, manages, and propagates an AI-native knowledge base across all the owner's projects. The KB is a neural network of JSON docs connected by tags, consumed by agents, maintained by scripts.
stack: Go CLI (cli/) — the leo command, built with cobra + charm/huh
JSON Knowledge Base (.claude/kb/) with schema validation
Bash scripts for zero-token deterministic work (validate, index, stale detection)
.leo/ directory per project — config, profiles, KB
Runtime adapters generate runtime-specific files (e.g., CLAUDE.md for Claude Code)
Claude Code hooks for automated KB maintenance
content.philosophy: Copilot-style, not Paperclip-style. Owner decides the what and why, Leo orchestrates (routing and delegation), Specialists execute. Token Economy: Opus thinks (Leo orchestration), Sonnet executes (Specialists), scripts handle deterministic work at zero cost. Knowledge is JSON for agents, human-readable outputs are generated on demand. Extend, never override.
constraints: All KB content is JSON — MD only exists as platform constraints (CLAUDE.md bootloader)
Core artifacts are English only — interaction language is personal choice (gitignored)
No rule change without explicit R2 from the owner
Leo ↔ Specialists model: Leo orchestrates with Opus, Specialists are ephemeral sub-agents running Sonnet
Scripts must never require AI tokens — if it's deterministic, it's a script
